title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary: The reviewer flagged two issues: (1) Step 4's script can take up to ~180s but the Chainsaw timeout is only 120s, so either tighten the loop bounds or increase the timeout. (2) The expected replica count is hardcoded as 3 — it should be read dynamically from `.spec.replicas` so the test stays valid if the count changes.
external_feedback:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The script in Step 4 can run up to ~180s in the worst case (60s for the first loop + 120s for the second) while the Chainsaw script timeout is set to 120s, so consider tightening the loop bounds or increasing the timeout to avoid the framework killing the script mid-recovery.
- The script currently hardcodes the expected replica count as 3 when checking readyReplicas; consider reading the desired replica count from the Deployment spec (e.g. `.spec.replicas`) so the test remains valid if the memcached replica count changes.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The script in Step 4 can run up to ~180s in the worst case (60s for the first loop + 120s for the second) while the Chainsaw script timeout is set to 120s, so consider tightening the loop bounds or increasing the timeout to avoid the framework killing the script mid-recovery.
- The script currently hardcodes the expected replica count as 3 when checking readyReplicas; consider reading the desired replica count from the Deployment spec (e.g. `.spec.replicas`) so the test remains valid if the memcached replica count changes.
~~~

</details>

***

resolution_summary: Both review comments were addressed in uncommitted changes to tests/e2e-chaos/memcached-pod-kill/chainsaw-test.yaml. The timeout/loop-bound mismatch was fixed by tightening Phase 1 from 30 to 15 iterations (30s max) and Phase 2 from 60 to 45 iterations (90s max), giving a 120s worst case, and the script timeout was increased from 120s to 150s to provide 30s margin. The hardcoded replica count of 3 was replaced by reading .spec.replicas from the Deployment into a DESIRED variable, with a guard clause validating the value. No changes were made to files outside the ones already modified on this branch.
